chapter_title.ar: بَابُ فَضْلِ الْحَجِّ وَ الْعُمْرَةِ وَ ثَوَابِهِمَا
chapter_title.en: Chapter 28 – Merits of the Hajj and the Umra, and their Rewards

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/136)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (7/136)
text: قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَشِيَّةً مِنَ الْعَشِيَّاتِ وَ نَحْنُ بِمِنًى وَ هُوَ يَحُثُّنِي عَلَى الْحَجِّ وَ يُرَغِّبُنِي فِيهِ يَا سَعِيدُ أَيُّمَا عَبْدٍ رَزَقَهُ اللَّهُ رِزْقاً مِنْ رِزْقِهِ فَأَخَذَ ذَلِكَ الرِّزْقَ فَأَنْفَقَهُ عَلَى نَفْسِهِ وَ عَلَى عِيَالِهِ ثُمَّ أَخْرَجَهُمْ قَدْ ضَحَاهُمْ بِالشَّمْسِ حَتَّى يَقْدَمَ بِهِمْ عَشِيَّةَ عَرَفَةَ إِلَى الْمَوْقِفِ فَيَقِيلَ أَ لَمْ تَرَ فُرَجاً تَكُونُ هُنَاكَ فِيهَا خَلَلٌ وَ لَيْسَ فِيهَا أَحَدٌ فَقُلْتُ بَلَى جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ

فَقَالَ يَجِي‏ءُ بِهِمْ قَدْ ضَحَاهُمْ حَتَّى يَشْعَبَ بِهِمْ تِلْكَ الْفُرَجَ فَيَقُولُ اللَّهُ تَبَارَكَ وَ تَعَالَى لَا شَرِيكَ لَهُ عَبْدِي رَزَقْتُهُ مِنْ رِزْقِي فَأَخَذَ ذَلِكَ الرِّزْقَ فَأَنْفَقَهُ فَضَحَى بِهِ نَفْسَهُ وَ عِيَالَهُ ثُمَّ جَاءَ بِهِمْ حَتَّى شَعَبَ بِهِمْ هَذِهِ الْفُرْجَةَ الْتِمَاسَ مَغْفِرَتِي أَغْفِرُ لَهُ ذَنْبَهُ وَ أَكْفِيهِ مَا أَهَمَّهُ وَ أَرْزُقُهُ
قَالَ سَعِيدٌ مَعَ أَشْيَاءَ قَالَهَا نَحْواً مِنْ عَشَرَةٍ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Umar Bin Hafs, from Saeed Bin Yasar who said,

‘Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, said during an evening from the evenings, when we were in Mina, and he<sup>asws</sup> was encouraging me upon the Hajj and making me desirous with regards to it: ‘O Saeed! Whichever servant is Graced by Allah<sup>azwj</sup> with sustenance from His<sup>azwj</sup> Sustenances, so he takes that sustenance and spends it upon himself and upon his dependents. Then he takes them out, having exposed them to the sun until he proceeds with them to an evening of Arafat, to the Pausing Stop. Do you see relief to be over there in a vacant spot, and there is no one in it?’ So I said, ‘Yes, may I be sacrificed for you<sup>asws</sup>’.

So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘He comes with them, having exposed them (to the sun) until he fills with them that relief, so Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Blessed and High, there being no associates for Him<sup>azwj</sup>, would be Saying: “My<sup>azwj</sup> servant, I<sup>azwj</sup> Graced him from My<sup>azwj</sup> Sustenance, so he took that sustenance and spent it, exposing himself and his dependents by it (to the hot sun), then he came over with them until he filled with them this relief, seeking My<sup>azwj</sup> Forgiveness. I<sup>azwj</sup> hereby Forgive his sins for him, and Suffice him for whatever worries him, and Sustain him’.
Saeed (the narrator) said, ‘Along with the things which he<sup>asws</sup> said, there were approximately ten’.
en.sarwar: 43. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Mu‘awiyah ibn ‘Ammar who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘When people take their places in Mina, an announcer announces, “Had you known in whose courtyard have you disembarked, you would have become certain of receiving compensation after having received forgiveness.’””
